stories.headline: China threatens retaliation if EU proceeds with 'Made in Europe' law
stories.summary: China is pushing EU member state capitals to scrap the European Commission's proposed 'Made in Europe' law, threatening countermeasures if national governments do not abandon the planned legislation. The move represents Beijing's latest attempt to fracture EU consensus by lobbying individual capitals rather than engaging Brussels directly.
stories.signal: China's divide-and-conquer lobbying strategy against EU industrial policy is intensifying; member states with high China trade exposure face significant pressure to defect from Commission positions.
stories.context: China's tech ambitions also increasingly tied to its property market recovery, per Nikkei Asia analysis.

stories.headline: Mali's Goita reappears as Russia claims it halted coup attempt
stories.summary: Mali's military leader Assimi Goita made his first public appearance following rebel attacks that had fuelled speculation about his grip on power, as Russia declared it had halted a coup attempt against its ally's government. Tuareg rebel spokespeople told Al Jazeera that the military government would fall 'sooner or later,' underscoring the fragility of the Russia-backed junta.
stories.signal: Russia's active intervention to preserve the Mali junta underscores the Wagner-successor network's deepening role in Sahel security; destabilisation risks remain high for the region's gold and uranium supply chains.
stories.context: Mali is a significant gold producer; instability affects junior mining equities and French-speaking West Africa broadly.

social_signals.text: As military government battles to reassert control, spokesperson for Tuareg rebels says it will fall 'sooner or later'.

stories.headline: Cognizant cuts 4,000 jobs under Project Leap AI restructuring
stories.summary: Cognizant announced it will cut 4,000 jobs as part of its 'Project Leap' restructuring, targeting $300 million in cost savings and margin expansion as the company lowers its full-year guidance amid a softer IT services demand environment. The layoffs reflect the broader tension in the IT outsourcing sector between AI-driven productivity gains and declining headcount demand from enterprise clients.
stories.signal: Cognizant's guidance cut and mass layoffs signal demand softness in discretionary IT spending is deepening; Indian IT sector employment and wage growth face structural headwinds.
stories.context: TCS and other Indian IT majors face similar AI disruption pressure; Cognizant has significant India delivery centre headcount.

stories.headline: AI boom doubles Samsung dynasty wealth to $45 billion in one year
stories.summary: The Lee family behind Samsung Electronics saw their combined wealth double to $45 billion in a single year, driven by the AI-fuelled semiconductor boom that sent Samsung's stock to new highs. The dynasty had previously faced a dual crisis of a multi-billion-dollar inheritance tax bill and the 2021 imprisonment of heir Jay Y. Lee.
stories.signal: Samsung family's wealth recovery signals AI-driven memory chip demand is overcoming earlier supply glut; watch for capex announcements on HBM and advanced packaging capacity.
stories.context: Samsung competing with SK Hynix on HBM supply to Nvidia; AI data centre buildout driving memory demand cycle.

stories.headline: Nvidia-linked Nevada data centre raises $4.59 billion in junk-bond sale
stories.summary: A Nevada data centre project tied to Nvidia Corp. raised $4.59 billion from a junk-bond sale, adding to a wave of high-yield deals funding artificial intelligence infrastructure. The bond sale reflects sustained investor appetite for AI infrastructure debt despite elevated credit risk, as hyperscaler demand continues to drive capacity buildout.
stories.signal: The scale of junk-rated AI infrastructure financing signals market confidence in long-term AI demand, but also builds leverage risk in the sector if monetisation timelines slip.
stories.context: Bloom Energy stock jumped 28% on data centre power demand; AI infrastructure spending cycle remains in early innings.

stories.headline: Belarus frees Polish journalist Poczobut as Lukashenko eyes Western ties
stories.summary: Belarus released journalist Andrzej Poczobut, a Polish-Belarusian reporter who had been imprisoned, as President Alexander Lukashenko signals a desire to improve relations with the West. The release is seen as a goodwill gesture amid broader European recalibration following the Ukraine war and shifting US engagement.
stories.signal: Lukashenko's Western overture—likely driven by economic isolation and changing Russian leverage dynamics—could open narrow diplomatic channels, though EU sanctions remain firmly in place.
stories.context: Hungary's Magyar separately targeting May deal to unlock €10B in frozen EU funds; Eastern European political dynamics shifting.

local_items.title: IPL: SRH chase 244! Head, Klaasen overshadow Rickelton’s historic MI ton in run-fest
local_items.summary: Travis Head and Heinrich Klaasen powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to a six-wicket victory over Mumbai Indians, successfully chasing a massive 244 with eight balls to spare. Their explosive batting overshadowed Ryan Rickelton's record-breaking unbeaten 123, which had propelled Mumbai to their highest tota

local_items.title: IPL 2026 Points Table: Klaasen Powers Hyderabad To Massive Chase Vs Mumbai; SRH Climb To 3rd, MI Stay 9th
local_items.summary: Klaasen scored 65* off 30 balls to help SRH seal a fifth win on the bounce in IPL 2026.

stories.headline: Trump rejects Iran peace offer, Hormuz shipping remains at trickle
stories.summary: The Trump administration rejected Iran's offer to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for lifting the US blockade, with Trump warning Iran to 'get smart' or face military action. UN Secretary-General warned the Hormuz standoff risks triggering a global food and energy emergency, while shipping traffic through the strait remains critically disrupted.
stories.signal: With no diplomatic off-ramp in sight and Hormuz effectively closed, global supply chain stress and oil price escalation will intensify through Q2 2026.
stories.context: Third month of active US-Iran-Israel military conflict; Strait of Hormuz blockade disrupting global fuel and food supply chains.

social_signals.text: Hormuz shipping traffic remains at a trickle as US-Iran deadlock deepens
'No more Mr Nice Guy': Trump warns Iran to 'get smart' over stalled talks

stories.headline: UAE quits OPEC effective May 1, stripping cartel of major producer
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates announced it will leave OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, removing one of the group's largest producers and significantly weakening the cartel's ability to manage global oil supply and prices. The move is seen as a major blow to Saudi Arabia's influence over the bloc and adds further uncertainty to already volatile oil markets disrupted by the Iran-Hormuz crisis.
stories.signal: UAE's exit, combined with the Hormuz blockade, creates a dual supply shock narrative that will keep Brent structurally elevated above $110 in the near term.
stories.context: UAE has long sought to pump above its OPEC quota; decision accelerated by geopolitical realignment amid Iran war.

stories.headline: WTI tops $106, Brent $117 as Hormuz blockade bites eighth day
stories.summary: US WTI crude surged 6.6% to $106.58 a barrel and Brent rose 5.9% to $117.81, extending gains into an eighth consecutive session after EIA data showed a sharp drop in US oil stockpiles and with no resolution to the Middle East crisis. Rising crude prices are sharply impacting India's import costs, pushing the rupee near all-time lows and stoking global inflation fears.
stories.signal: Sustained crude above $100 will force central banks — including the RBI — to hold rates longer, suppressing rate-cut expectations globally.
stories.context: Oil rally driven by Hormuz closure, UAE OPEC exit, and tight EIA inventory data; Iran war now in third month.

social_signals.text: Natural gas is so abundant in West Texas that prices have plummeted below zero, creating an economic advantage for the US as the Iran war chokes global supplies

stories.headline: Exit polls show BJP sweep Assam, Bengal knife-edge, TVK shock Tamil Nadu
stories.summary: Exit polls across four major Indian state elections project the BJP-led NDA sweeping Assam with 70-80 seats, holding a wafer-thin edge over TMC in West Bengal with a 142-seat projection, and the Congress-led UDF defeating the LDF in Kerala after a decade. The most dramatic prediction is in Tamil Nadu, where the NDTV-Axis My India poll projects actor Vijay's TVK winning 98-120 seats, potentially upending the DMK-NDA contest entirely.
stories.signal: A TVK breakthrough in Tamil Nadu and a BJP win in Bengal would represent the most significant Indian political realignment in a decade, with major implications for 2029 national election calculus.
stories.context: Assembly elections held across Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Kerala, Assam and Puducherry; results to be declared shortly.

stories.headline: Fed set to hold rates in likely Powell's final policy meeting
stories.summary: The US Federal Reserve's two-day FOMC meeting concludes Wednesday with Jerome Powell widely expected to hold rates unchanged; markets are watching the tone of Powell's statement closely as it may be his last as Fed chair. Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee for Fed chair, cleared the Senate Banking Committee on a party-line vote, advancing to a full Senate confirmation vote.
stories.signal: Powell's hawkish or dovish tone on inflation — particularly in the context of oil-driven price pressures — will set the trajectory for rate expectations through summer 2026.
stories.context: Trump has been pressuring Powell to cut rates; Warsh nomination signals imminent leadership change at the Fed.

social_signals.text: Fed decision live: US central bank set to hold rates at what could be Powell's last meeting
Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee for chairman of the Federal Reserve, heads to a full Senate vote after he was approved by the Senate Banking Committee.

stories.headline: Pentagon confirms Iran war has cost US $25 billion so far
stories.summary: A senior Pentagon official confirmed for the first time that the US military conflict with Iran has cost approximately $25 billion, as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth defended a proposed $1.5 trillion military budget before Congress and pressed Iran to abandon nuclear ambitions as part of any future deal. The disclosure came as AP reported Israeli strikes crippled thousands of Iranian factories, with hundreds of thousands of Iranians losing jobs.
stories.signal: The $25B figure — likely understated — signals the Iran war is already straining US fiscal capacity and will intensify Congressional debate over the defense budget.
stories.context: First official US government estimate of Iran war costs; Hegseth simultaneously requesting largest peacetime defense budget in US history.

stories.headline: Gulf leaders hold first post-Iran war summit in Jeddah, back unified stance
stories.summary: Gulf Cooperation Council leaders convened in Jeddah for their first high-level summit since the start of the US-Iran war, with Qatar's emir declaring a 'unified Gulf stance' toward the conflict. The meeting came as the UAE simultaneously exited OPEC and Abu Dhabi received Russia's Deputy Prime Minister, signalling Gulf states are navigating complex diplomatic balancing acts between the US-led coalition and Russia-China axis.
stories.signal: The GCC's declared unity masks divergent interests — UAE's OPEC exit and UAE-Russia meetings suggest Gulf states are hedging against a prolonged conflict rather than fully aligning with Washington.
stories.context: UAE also received Bahrain's king and Russia's deputy PM in Abu Dhabi, underscoring multi-vector Gulf diplomacy during the Iran conflict.

stories.headline: Iranian rial hits new low as US blockade, strikes shatter economy
stories.summary: Iran's currency fell to a new historic low as US and Israeli airstrikes crippled thousands of factories, with hundreds of thousands of Iranians already unemployed and millions more at risk. The blockade has virtually halted Iran's oil exports, nearly filled its storage capacity, and AP investigation found Israeli strikes specifically targeted Iran's steel and industrial base.
stories.signal: Iran's economic collapse accelerates pressure on Tehran to negotiate, but domestic hardliners may exploit the crisis to consolidate power — reducing the likelihood of a near-term deal.
stories.context: Iran's oil storage reportedly near capacity with nowhere to export; rial at all-time lows.

social_signals.text: Iran's currency falls to new low as US blockade, sanctions impact trade
Is Iran's oil storage nearly full – and will it have to cut production?

stories.headline: Rupee falls to 94.82, near all-time low on surging crude costs
stories.summary: The Indian rupee fell 14 paise to close at 94.82 against the US dollar, near its all-time low, as elevated crude oil prices sharply impact India's import bill amid the ongoing West Asia crisis. Forex traders flagged that the Hormuz blockade-driven oil surge is compounding investor anxiety and weighing on India's current account dynamics heading into the US Fed decision.
stories.signal: With crude above $100 and the rupee near record lows, the RBI faces a difficult trade-off between defending the currency and supporting growth — watch for possible FX intervention signals.
stories.context: India imports approximately 85% of its crude oil needs; every $10 rise in Brent adds ~$15 billion to annual import costs.

stories.headline: Sensex jumps 609 pts, Nifty at 24,177 on FMCG and auto gains
stories.summary: The BSE Sensex rebounded 609 points (0.79%) to close at 77,496 and the NSE Nifty rose 182 points (0.76%) to 24,177, driven by gains in FMCG and auto sectors as Asian markets also rallied. The recovery came despite rupee weakness and crude oil concerns, with markets pricing in the Fed's expected rate hold and awaiting US mega-cap tech earnings.
stories.signal: The rebound is technically fragile — sustained crude above $110 and a weak rupee could reverse gains quickly if the Fed signals delayed cuts.
stories.context: Indian markets recovering from recent volatility driven by West Asia war and oil spike; domestic election exit poll results also in focus.

stories.headline: Bajaj Finance Q4 profit surges 22% to ₹5,553 crore, AUM tops ₹5 trillion
stories.summary: Bajaj Finance reported a 22% year-on-year jump in Q4 profit to ₹5,553 crore, crossing ₹5 trillion in assets under management as AI-driven efficiencies — including a sixfold rise in peak-day loan processing — began reflecting in operations. The company declared a ₹6 per share final dividend, signalling management confidence in sustained growth momentum into FY27.
stories.signal: Bajaj Finance's AUM milestone and AI-driven cost efficiency make it a bellwether for India's consumer credit cycle — strong numbers reduce near-term NBFC sector stress concerns.
stories.context: Bajaj Finance is India's largest NBFC by market cap; results are a key indicator of urban consumption and credit health.

stories.headline: King Charles meets Trump, hails US-UK bond amid special relationship strain
stories.summary: King Charles III arrived in Washington for a four-day state visit, meeting President Trump to reaffirm the US-UK 'special relationship' at a low point in bilateral ties, with Trump praising the 'cherished' bond. The visit was complicated by the revelation that the UK's US ambassador made private remarks downplaying the relationship, which the British government quickly distanced itself from.
stories.signal: Charles claiming Trump agreed with him on banning Iranian nuclear weapons — if confirmed — could signal a diplomatic channel outside formal government negotiations.
stories.context: UK-US relations strained by Trump tariffs, ambassador leak, and divergences over Iran war strategy.

social_signals.text: King Charles agrees with me on Iran nuclear weapon ban, claims Trump

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank, Santander, UBS post record profits amid Iran war volatility
stories.summary: Three of Europe's largest banks — Deutsche Bank, Santander, and UBS — delivered record or above-forecast profits in Q1 2026, with trading desks benefiting from elevated volatility generated by the US-Iran war. TotalEnergies also posted strong earnings with net profit up 51%, prompting political calls in France for windfall taxes on energy superprofits.
stories.signal: European bank outperformance driven by war-related trading revenues is unsustainable and masks underlying credit risk from a slowing economy — watch for asset quality deterioration in H2.
stories.context: TotalEnergies profit up 51% year-on-year; European energy companies benefiting from Hormuz-driven price spike.

stories.headline: Airbus Q1 profit halves as aircraft deliveries hit 17-year low
stories.summary: Airbus reported a 50% drop in first-quarter profit as aircraft deliveries slowed to the lowest level since 2009, with supply chain bottlenecks continuing to undermine the European manufacturer's production ramp. The results come at a particularly difficult time as rival Boeing recovers manufacturing momentum, threatening Airbus's recent market share gains.
stories.signal: Airbus's supply chain failure combined with Boeing's recovery creates a rare competitive window for Boeing to recapture airline orders — watch for order announcements at upcoming airshows.
stories.context: Airbus has struggled with engine shortages and supply chain delays since 2023; Iran war disrupting aluminum and titanium supply chains.

stories.headline: Pakistan-Afghanistan truce strains after university strike kills civilians
stories.summary: Afghanistan accused Pakistan of launching cross-border attacks killing four and injuring 70, including 30 students at a university in Kunar province, calling it a 'war crime' as Pakistan denied involvement. The incident has cast a shadow over a fragile ceasefire and ongoing peace talks between the two countries, reigniting tensions that had briefly subsided.
stories.signal: If verified, targeting of civilian educational infrastructure signals a dangerous escalation that could collapse the ceasefire and drag regional powers including China and India deeper into the dispute.
stories.context: Pakistan-Afghanistan relations have been volatile since Taliban takeover; both countries host significant militant groups targeting each other.

stories.headline: Vedanta demerger price discovery Thursday; FY26 revenue beats at ₹1.74 lakh crore
stories.summary: Vedanta will undergo a price discovery session from 9:15-9:45am on April 30 ahead of its demerger, with normal trading resuming at ex-demerged price from 10am. The company reported FY26 revenue of ₹1,74,075 crore — surpassing Bloomberg estimates of ₹1,70,000 crore — driven by strong commodity prices, with analysts debating the fair value split between demerged entities.
stories.signal: Vedanta's revenue beat amid high commodity prices sets a positive base for the demerged entities, but watch for volatility during price discovery as the market reprices each business segment independently.
stories.context: Vedanta's demerger separates its oil, zinc, aluminum, and iron ore businesses into distinct listed entities.

stories.headline: Global gold demand value hits record $193bn in Q1 2026, up 74% YoY
stories.summary: Global gold demand rose 2% in volume in Q1 2026 on strong bar and coin buying, but the value of demand surged to a record $193 billion — up 74% year-on-year — according to the World Gold Council, reflecting the extraordinary price environment. Meanwhile, Comex gold slid $86 to $4,522 per ounce as rising oil prices heightened inflation concerns and pressured expectations for rate cuts.
stories.signal: Gold at $4,500+ reflects structural safe-haven demand from the Iran war and dollar uncertainty; a pullback is technically overdue but fundamentals support elevated floor prices.
stories.context: Gold has surged significantly in 2026 driven by Iran war uncertainty, central bank buying, and dollar weakness.

stories.headline: Germany eyes emergency borrowing as Iran war strains economy
stories.summary: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil said he does not rule out invoking emergency borrowing provisions that would allow the government to bypass constitutional debt limits, citing the economic strain from what he termed 'Trump's irresponsible war' in Iran. The move would mark a significant shift in Germany's fiscal orthodoxy, which has already been loosened for defense spending.
stories.signal: Germany breaking its debt brake for war-related economic emergency spending would be a historic precedent with lasting implications for EU fiscal rules and Eurozone bond markets.
stories.context: Germany already suspended its debt brake for defense in 2025; this would extend emergency borrowing to economic relief.

stories.headline: Over 1.2 million in Lebanon face acute hunger as Israel war continues
stories.summary: A UN-backed report warned that over 1.2 million people in Lebanon — a quarter of the population — face acute hunger due to Israel's ongoing war operations, as Israeli officers described the mission as focused on 'systematic destruction' of buildings. Three women were killed in an Israeli air raid on south Lebanon's Tayr Debba village, with Lebanese health authorities confirming at least 11 more wounded.
stories.signal: Lebanon's humanitarian deterioration into a famine-risk scenario will intensify international pressure for a ceasefire, potentially creating a diplomatic lever separate from the Iran-Hormuz negotiations.
stories.context: Israel has been conducting operations in Lebanon since March 2026; UN food agencies warn of imminent famine conditions.

stories.headline: Cognizant cuts 4,000 jobs under Project Leap, lowers full-year guidance
stories.summary: Cognizant announced it will cut 4,000 jobs as part of its Project Leap restructuring program targeting $300 million in savings, even as the company lowered its full-year revenue guidance amid a softer demand environment. The layoffs reflect broader IT sector headwinds as AI-driven automation reduces headcount requirements while simultaneously creating pressure to upskill remaining workforce.
stories.signal: Cognizant's guidance cut is a leading indicator for the broader Indian IT outsourcing sector — watch for similar moves from Wipro and HCL Tech in upcoming results.
stories.context: Indian IT sector under pressure from AI automation, US demand slowdown, and visa restrictions; Cognizant among most US-revenue-exposed firms.

stories.headline: Seven killed in Bengaluru as hospital compound wall collapses in rain
stories.summary: Seven people including three children were killed when a compound wall near Bowring Hospital in Bengaluru collapsed following heavy pre-monsoon rains, with Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah visiting the site and announcing ₹5 lakh compensation for bereaved families. The tragedy highlights urban infrastructure vulnerabilities in India's tech capital as the monsoon season approaches.
stories.signal: The Bengaluru disaster will intensify scrutiny of urban infrastructure compliance in rapidly developing Indian cities and could accelerate regulatory action on construction safety.
stories.context: Pre-monsoon rains have been unusually intense in Karnataka; Bengaluru has seen multiple infrastructure failures in recent years.

stories.headline: All-weather Vande Bharat to connect Jammu-Srinagar under 5 hours from May 2
stories.summary: India will launch an all-weather Vande Bharat Express service connecting Jammu Tawi to Srinagar in under five hours from May 2, running through the newly completed Udhampur-Srinagar-Baramulla Rail Link — a project decades in the making through challenging Himalayan terrain. The train features specially engineered designs for high-altitude and extreme weather operations, marking a historic connectivity milestone for Kashmir.
stories.signal: The Jammu-Srinagar rail link is as much a strategic connectivity asset as a civilian infrastructure win — it reduces Kashmir's dependence on the weather-vulnerable Jammu-Srinagar highway.
stories.context: The USBRL project is one of India's most complex railway engineering feats; strategically significant for military logistics and civilian access to Kashmir.

stories.headline: ADB cuts Asia growth forecast to 4.7% as West Asia disruptions deepen
stories.summary: The Asian Development Bank cut its growth outlook for Asia to 4.7%, citing deepening disruptions from the West Asia crisis including oil price shocks, supply chain strains from the Hormuz blockade, and the broader impact of the US-Iran war on regional trade. The downgrade affects major developing economies dependent on stable energy imports and export demand.
stories.signal: The ADB downgrade confirms the Iran war's macro contagion is now being formally priced into institutional forecasts — expect World Bank and IMF to follow with similar revisions.
stories.context: ADB had previously projected 5.1% growth for developing Asia in 2026; revision reflects oil shock and trade route disruption.

stories.headline: BOJ rate hike groundwork crumbles as geopolitics, government pressure mount
stories.summary: Nikkei reports that the Bank of Japan's careful preparation for further rate hikes has been disrupted by the prolonged Middle East crisis and growing recession fears within the Japanese government, with the Takaichi administration applying pressure to hold rates. The BOJ now faces a stagflation dilemma — delaying hikes risks entrenching inflation, but raising rates into a slowdown could deepen economic pain.
stories.signal: BOJ's June meeting has become a pivotal inflection point; failure to hike could weaken the yen further, importing additional inflation through higher energy costs.
stories.context: BOJ had been signalling gradual rate normalization; Iran war has complicated its communication strategy significantly.

social_signals.text: 崩れた日銀利上げの「地ならし」　ちらつく高市政権の圧力 — BOJ rate hike groundwork collapses amid Middle East crisis; stagflation risk rising for June meeting

local_items.title: IPL 2026: Ryan Rickelton scripts history with fastest-ever MI hundred
local_items.summary: Ryan Rickelton made a sensational return to the Mumbai Indians playing XI, smashing the fastest century in franchise history during their IPL 2026 match against Sunrisers Hyderabad. The South African opener reached his maiden IPL hundred in just 44 balls, a blistering knock featuring seven sixes and

local_items.title: CM asks Uttam to write to Kishan Reddy on Tummidihatti barrage
local_items.summary: Seeks Centre’s intervention to persuade Maharashtra; geo-testing at Medigadda to be expedited

stories.headline: Trump rejects Iran peace offer, warns military action remains on table
stories.summary: The Trump administration rejected Iran's proposal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz in exchange for lifting the US blockade, with Trump warning Tehran to 'get smart' or face further military action. A Pentagon official confirmed the war has cost the US $25 billion so far, while Defense Secretary Hegseth pushed Iran to abandon nuclear ambitions in Congressional testimony.
stories.signal: With Trump rejecting Iran's opening bid and the Hormuz blockade holding, oil supply disruptions and global economic pressure are set to intensify.
stories.context: GCC summit in Jeddah signals unified Gulf stance; UN warns Hormuz blockade risks global food emergency

social_signals.text: 'No more Mr Nice Guy': Trump warns Iran to 'get smart' over stalled talks
Hormuz shipping traffic remains at a trickle as US-Iran deadlock deepens

stories.headline: UAE quits OPEC effective May 1, stripping cartel of major producer
stories.summary: The United Arab Emirates announced it will leave both OPEC and OPEC+ effective May 1, removing one of the cartel's largest producers and signaling Abu Dhabi's intent to pump oil on its own terms. The move is seen as a major blow to Saudi Arabia's dominance of the cartel and adds further uncertainty to global oil supply dynamics already stressed by the Iran conflict.
stories.signal: UAE's exit accelerates OPEC's fragmentation and could trigger a supply surge that partially offsets Iran war-driven price spikes.
stories.context: Brent crude futures eased 1% to $111.25/bbl on the news but remain elevated on Iran conflict premium

stories.headline: Exit polls show BJP sweep Assam, BJPedge Bengal, TVK shock in Tamil Nadu
stories.summary: Exit polls across four Indian states show the BJP projected to retain Assam with 70-80 seats and hold a wafer-thin edge in West Bengal with ~142 seats, potentially unseating Mamata Banerjee's TMC. In Tamil Nadu, NDTV-Axis My India's outlier poll projects actor Vijay's TVK winning 98-120 seats in a potential Kejriwal-like breakthrough, while UDF is predicted to oust the LDF in Kerala after 10 years.
stories.signal: A BJP gain in West Bengal would be the most consequential political shift in India since 2014, while a TVK surge in Tamil Nadu would redraw southern opposition politics.
stories.context: West Bengal recorded a record 92.47% turnout across both phases; exit polls carry significant uncertainty

stories.headline: Israeli strikes cripple Iran factories; hundreds of thousands lose jobs
stories.summary: US and Israeli airstrikes have destroyed thousands of Iranian factories, with hundreds of thousands already unemployed and millions more at risk, according to AP reporting. Iran's currency has fallen to a new low as the US blockade and sanctions compound trade disruption, while Tehran residents face deep economic uncertainty under a fragile truce.
stories.signal: Iran's economic collapse could force a more desperate diplomatic posture or internal political crisis within months.
stories.context: Iran's oil storage reportedly near capacity as exports are blocked

social_signals.text: Iran's currency falls to new low as US blockade, sanctions impact trade
Is Iran's oil storage nearly full – and will it have to cut production?
Tehran's residents are living through a fragile truce and deep economic uncertainty.

stories.headline: Oil above $111 as Hormuz blockade, Iran war compress global supply
stories.summary: Brent crude has climbed for seven consecutive sessions, hitting $111.25 a barrel before easing slightly on UAE's OPEC exit news, as the Strait of Hormuz blockade keeps shipping at a trickle. The Iran war is simultaneously squeezing global jet fuel supplies, threatening airline summer schedules and driving fare increases.
stories.signal: Sustained triple-digit oil with a blocked Hormuz creates a structural inflation shock that central banks cannot easily counter with rate policy.
stories.context: West Texas natural gas prices have plummeted below zero due to abundance, creating a US competitive advantage

social_signals.text: The Iran war has squeezed global supplies of jet fuel, threatening to upend summer vacations as airlines cancel flights and increase airfares.
Natural gas is so abundant in West Texas that prices have plummeted below zero, creating an economic advantage for the US as the Iran war chokes global supplies.

stories.headline: Fed holds rates as Powell delivers likely final policy decision
stories.summary: The US Federal Reserve's two-day FOMC meeting concludes with Jerome Powell set to announce the rate decision in what is expected to be his last policy announcement as Fed Chair. Markets are pricing in no change to rates, but the tone of Powell's guidance amid elevated oil prices and Iran war inflation pressures will be closely scrutinized.
stories.signal: Powell's swan song statement on inflation and the Iran war's economic impact will set the tone for Warsh's incoming Fed chairmanship.
stories.context: Kevin Warsh cleared Senate Banking Committee confirmation along party lines ahead of full Senate vote

social_signals.text: Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee for chairman of the Federal Reserve, heads to a full Senate vote after he was approved by the Senate Banking Committee.

stories.headline: Bengal records 92.47% turnout, highest since Independence amid BJP-TMC clash
stories.summary: West Bengal completed its two-phase assembly elections with a record 92.47% aggregate turnout, the highest the state has seen since Independence, signaling extraordinary voter mobilization. Mamata Banerjee accused BJP of attempting to rig elections at her Bhabanipur constituency, alleging CAPF atrocities, while exit polls show BJP holding a wafer-thin edge of around 142 seats.
stories.signal: Record turnout in an intensely contested Bengal election raises the stakes for a potential historic transfer of power from the TMC.
stories.context: Poll of polls gives BJP a razor-thin 142-seat projection in a 294-seat house

stories.headline: Bajaj Finance Q4 profit surges 22% to ₹5,553 crore; AUM crosses ₹5 trillion
stories.summary: Bajaj Finance reported a 22% year-on-year rise in consolidated net profit to ₹5,553 crore for Q4 FY26, with net interest income growing 20% to ₹11,781 crore, while assets under management crossed the ₹5 trillion milestone. The company declared a ₹6 per share final dividend and cited AI-driven efficiencies including a sixfold rise in peak-day loan processing capacity.
stories.signal: Bajaj Finance's AUM milestone and AI adoption signal continued NBFC sector strength even as macro headwinds from the Iran war build.
stories.context: Results slightly below street estimates on PAT basis but strong on AUM growth and AI integration narrative

stories.headline: King Charles meets Trump in Washington to repair US-UK special relationship
stories.summary: King Charles III arrived in Washington for a four-day state visit, meeting President Trump who praised the 'cherished' US-UK bond, even as leaked remarks from the UK's US ambassador appeared to downplay the relationship's standing. The UK government distanced itself from the envoy's comments, while Trump claimed Charles agreed with him on banning Iran from obtaining nuclear weapons.
stories.signal: The state visit is a diplomatic reset attempt at a low point in bilateral relations, with the Iran nuclear question now a new point of transatlantic alignment.
stories.context: UK ambassador's leaked private remarks created diplomatic embarrassment ahead of the visit

social_signals.text: King Charles agrees with me on Iran nuclear weapon ban, claims Trump

stories.headline: Rupee falls to 94.82 per dollar, near all-time low on crude surge
stories.summary: The Indian rupee weakened 14 paise to close at 94.82 against the US dollar, approaching its all-time low, as elevated crude oil prices above $111/bbl sharply increased import cost projections. Forex traders said the ongoing West Asia crisis and potential for wider conflict are fuelling investor anxiety alongside the US Fed rate decision watch.
stories.signal: A rupee near record lows combined with triple-digit crude creates a twin deficit pressure that could force the RBI into emergency FX intervention.
stories.context: Sensex rebounded 609 points to 77,496 on FMCG and auto buying despite currency pressure

stories.headline: Gulf leaders meet Jeddah summit first time since Iran war began
stories.summary: Gulf Cooperation Council leaders convened in Jeddah for their first high-level summit since the start of the US-Iran war, with Qatar's Emir describing the gathering as embodying a 'unified Gulf stance' toward the conflict. The meeting comes amid UAE's OPEC exit and Abu Dhabi's growing strategic independence from Riyadh.
stories.signal: A unified Gulf diplomatic posture could become a significant mediating channel between Washington and Tehran if direct talks remain deadlocked.
stories.context: UAE also received Russian Deputy PM in Abu Dhabi, signaling multi-directional Gulf diplomacy

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank, Santander, UBS post record profits amid Iran war volatility
stories.summary: Three of Europe's largest banks — Deutsche Bank, Santander, and UBS — delivered record or above-forecast quarterly profits driven by elevated market volatility and trading revenues stemming from the Iran war. TotalEnergies also posted strong earnings from high oil prices, while Mercedes-Benz struck a more cautious tone due to collapsing China sales.
stories.signal: European bank trading desks are the unexpected winners of Iran war volatility, creating a divergence between financial sector performance and the real economy.
stories.context: Airbus Q1 profit dropped by half as aircraft deliveries hit lowest since 2009, contrasting with bank earnings

stories.headline: Pakistan-Afghanistan ceasefire frays after university strike kills civilians
stories.summary: Tensions between Pakistan and Afghanistan have resumed sharply after Kabul accused Islamabad of launching cross-border attacks that killed four people and injured 70 including 30 students, calling the university strike a 'war crime.' Pakistan denied involvement but the incidents have cast a shadow over an existing ceasefire and ongoing peace talks.
stories.signal: A collapse of the Pakistan-Afghanistan truce would create a second active conflict zone in South Asia at a time when India-Pakistan tensions are already elevated.
stories.context: Civilian casualties in Kunar province are the flashpoint; Taliban government has made formal accusations

stories.headline: Airbus profit halves as Q1 deliveries hit 17-year low
stories.summary: Airbus reported a 50% drop in first-quarter profit as aircraft deliveries slowed to their lowest level since 2009, driven by persistent supply chain bottlenecks in engines and aerostructures. The crisis is occurring at a strategically damaging moment as rival Boeing accelerates its manufacturing recovery.
stories.signal: Airbus's supply chain crisis at peak demand creates a multi-year delivery backlog risk that may force airline fleet planning revisions globally.
stories.context: European aviation sector also faces jet fuel squeeze from Hormuz blockade

social_signals.text: Porsche's profit drops by more than a fifth in first quarter

stories.headline: Vedanta demerger price discovery Thursday; FY26 revenue beats estimates
stories.summary: Vedanta's shares will undergo a price discovery session on April 30 ahead of formal demerger trading, with experts weighing in on expected ex-demerger pricing. The company reported a strong FY26 with revenue jumping 15.83% to ₹1,74,075 crore, beating Bloomberg estimates by approximately ₹4,000 crore on strong commodity prices and record volumes.
stories.signal: Vedanta demerger execution will be a key test of India's market infrastructure for large-scale corporate restructurings.
stories.context: Vedanta's FY26 beat was driven by elevated zinc, aluminium and oil commodity prices

stories.headline: Cognizant cuts 4,000 jobs targeting $300M savings as AI reshapes IT demand
stories.summary: Cognizant announced 4,000 job cuts under its Project Leap restructuring program targeting $300 million in annual savings, even as the company lowered its full-year revenue guidance amid softening IT services demand. The cuts reflect dual pressure from AI-driven automation reducing headcount requirements and weakening enterprise spending.
stories.signal: Cognizant's cuts signal Indian IT services sector is entering a structural headcount reduction cycle that will affect India's white-collar employment market.
stories.context: TCS and other Indian IT majors are watching sector-wide demand signals from US enterprise clients

stories.headline: German finance minister eyes emergency borrowing as Iran war bites economy
stories.summary: Germany's Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil declined to rule out invoking an emergency declaration to bypass the constitutional debt brake, citing economic damage from 'Trump's irresponsible war' against Iran. Such a declaration would allow deficit spending beyond the constitutionally mandated limits, potentially unlocking billions in economic support.
stories.signal: Germany invoking emergency fiscal powers over the Iran war would represent the most significant breach of its debt brake framework in years, reshaping European fiscal dynamics.
stories.context: Merz government is simultaneously deepening military ties with Washington while publicly sparring with Trump over Iran

stories.headline: Over 1.2 million Lebanese face acute hunger; Israeli strikes kill civilians
stories.summary: A UN-backed report found more than 1.2 million people in Lebanon are expected to face acute hunger due to Israel's ongoing war, with The National reporting a quarter of Lebanon's population affected. Israeli air raids on southern Lebanon continue, with three women killed in a strike on Tayr Debba village, while Israeli officers reportedly described their Lebanon mission as focused on 'systematic destruction' of buildings.
stories.signal: Lebanon's food security crisis risks triggering a mass displacement emergency that could destabilize neighboring Jordan and Syria.
stories.context: Gaza also faces severe power cuts destroying livelihoods and healthcare infrastructure

stories.headline: ADB slashes Asia growth forecast to 4.7% on West Asia disruptions
stories.summary: The Asian Development Bank cut its growth outlook for Asia to 4.7%, citing deepening disruptions from the West Asia conflict including oil price shocks, supply chain stress, and reduced trade flows through the Strait of Hormuz. The downgrade reflects broad-based exposure across South and Southeast Asian economies to elevated energy import costs.
stories.signal: A 4.7% ADB growth ceiling for Asia under current conditions will compress sovereign fiscal space and corporate earnings across the region for the remainder of 2026.
stories.context: Thailand held rates at 1% as expected, reflecting cautious regional monetary stance

stories.headline: Global gold demand value hits record $193B in Q1 2026; India buying strong
stories.summary: The World Gold Council reported global gold demand rose 2% in volume terms in January-March 2026, but the value of demand surged to a record $193 billion, up 74% year-on-year, driven by strong bar and coin buying amid geopolitical uncertainty. Comex gold has since eased $86/oz to $4,522 ahead of the US Fed decision as oil price surge raises inflation concerns.
stories.signal: Gold's record value demand despite modest volume growth confirms structural safe-haven reallocation from financial assets to physical gold.
stories.context: Gold at $4,522 represents a massive premium to pre-conflict levels; silver also pulling back to $71.71

stories.headline: 7 killed including 3 children as hospital wall collapses in Bengaluru rains
stories.summary: Unseasonal heavy rains in Bengaluru caused a compound wall near Bowring Hospital to collapse, killing seven people including three children. Karnataka Chief Minister Siddaramaiah visited the site and announced ₹5 lakh compensation for families of the deceased.
stories.signal: The Bengaluru disaster highlights chronic urban infrastructure vulnerabilities in India's tech capital as extreme weather events intensify.
stories.context: Karnataka government facing pressure over urban infrastructure maintenance in Bengaluru

stories.headline: All-weather Vande Bharat from Jammu to Srinagar under 5 hours from May 2
stories.summary: India will launch an all-weather Vande Bharat Express connecting Jammu to Srinagar in under five hours from May 2, running on the newly completed Udhampur-Srinagar-Baramulla Rail Link. The route includes the world's highest railway bridge over the Chenab river and will significantly cut travel time from the current road journey of 8-10 hours.
stories.signal: The Vande Bharat USBRL launch is a major strategic connectivity milestone for Jammu & Kashmir, with economic and security implications.
stories.context: The Chenab bridge is among the world's highest rail bridges; project decades in the making

stories.headline: Indian military drone exports entering multifold growth phase: Nikkei
stories.summary: India's military drone export industry is entering a phase of exponential growth as global demand for battle-tested unmanned systems surges against the backdrop of multiple active conflicts, according to Nikkei Asia. Indian manufacturers are positioned to capitalize on export interest from Southeast Asia, the Middle East, and Africa.
stories.signal: India's drone export momentum could establish it as a tier-2 defense supplier globally within the next three years, complementing its existing missile and munitions trade.
stories.context: Zelenskyy also opening door to Ukrainian arms exports, creating new competition in the global defense market

stories.topic: Bank of Japan Rate Hike Pressure
stories.headline: BOJ rate hike groundwork crumbles under Iran war, Takaichi government pressure
stories.summary: The Bank of Japan's carefully prepared path toward interest rate normalization has been disrupted by prolonged Middle East turmoil and growing pressure from the Takaichi government concerned about recession risks, according to Nikkei reporting. The BOJ now faces a stagflation dilemma — delaying hikes risks entrenching inflation, but hiking risks exacerbating an already slowing economy.
stories.signal: BOJ's June meeting is shaping up as a critical juncture where political pressure and economic reality will clash with the central bank's inflation mandate.
stories.context: Nikkei analysis notes Iran war duration was an 'unforeseen miscalculation' in BOJ's forward guidance

stories.headline: Finnish Kone acquires German rival TKE in €29.4bn elevator mega-deal
stories.summary: Finnish lift manufacturer Kone has acquired German competitor ThyssenKrupp Elevator (TKE) in a €29.4 billion deal, nearly doubling its size in what Finnish media are calling the largest corporate acquisition ever completed by a Finnish company. The deal reshapes the global elevator and escalator industry.
stories.signal: The Kone-TKE merger creates a European industrial champion in building infrastructure, with significant implications for construction sector supply chains globally.
stories.context: Deal comes as European industrials face headwinds from Iran war supply chain disruptions

stories.headline: China suspends new autonomous driving permits after Baidu incident
stories.summary: Chinese regulators have suspended the issuance of new autonomous driving permits following an undisclosed incident involving Baidu's robotaxi operations, according to Nikkei Asia. The suspension signals a regulatory pullback in one of the world's most aggressive autonomous vehicle testing markets.
stories.signal: China's AV permit freeze will delay commercialization timelines for domestic players and may give Western competitors a temporary reprieve in the global robotaxi race.
stories.context: China had previously been the most permissive major market for AV testing and commercial deployment

local_items.title: IPL 2026: Ryan Rickelton scripts history with fastest-ever MI hundred
local_items.summary: Ryan Rickelton made a sensational return to the Mumbai Indians playing XI, smashing the fastest century in franchise history during their IPL 2026 match against Sunrisers Hyderabad.
